title: React Lists and Keys Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of array mapping and the importance of unique keys in React.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the best JavaScript function to use for rendering an array of elements in React?
options: forEach()
filter()
map()
reduce()

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'key' prop in a React list elements?
options: To provide a label for users.
To help React identify which items have changed, been added, or been removed.
To specify the CSS class of each list item.
To count the number of items in the list.

questions.question: Do keys need to be globally unique across the whole application?
options: Yes, always.
No, they only need to be unique among siblings.
Yes, but only if you use state management.
No, they can repeat anywhere.

questions.question: When is it generally discouraged to use the array index as a key in React list elements?
options: When the list order may change (sorting, adding/removing).
When the list contains more than 10 items.
When you're using class components.
Never, using the index is always recommended.

questions.question: Is the key prop passed as a regular prop into the child component (e.g., props.key)?
options: Yes, like any other prop.
No, it's a special prop used only by React and not accessible within the component.
Only if you de-structure it.
Only in older versions of React.
